reasoning:
The question asks whether the 4-week moving average of initial unemployment claims (IC4WSA) will exceed its 2025-10-26 value on three future dates. The actual FRED value for the week ending 2025-10-25 was 226,750. 

For the first date (2025-11-02): The calculation replaces the week ending Oct 4 (which reverse-engineers to an elevated ~240k) with the week of Nov 1. Because recent weekly claims have averaged ~222k (219k, 232k, 216k), the incoming week is highly likely to be lower than the ~240k dropping out. Thus, the moving average is mathematically very likely to fall. Probability = 25%.

For 2025-11-25: This corresponds to the moving average for the week ending Nov 22, dropping all October values. To exceed 226.75k, November's base claims level must rise slightly from its late-October run rate (~222k). Downward seasonality and momentum suggest this leans NO, but uncertainty accumulates over 4 weeks. Probability = 38%.

For 2026-01-24: At 3 months out, economic trends hold significantly more sway. With the Sahm rule triggered and the labor market mildly softening, creeping past 226.75k (a structurally middling figure in recent years) approaches a coin toss. Representing increased uncertainty horizon, Probability = 48%.
batch_queries: Will the 4-week moving average of initial unemployment claims have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-10-26?
